title: BookDocument
description: Canonical shape for an ingested book — the single hand-written source of truth. lib/book/schema.ts (Zod) is generated from this file via json-schema-to-zod; /ingestion (Python) validates against this file directly via jsonschema. Do not hand-edit either of those; edit this file. See ingestion.md.

sections.description: Content-first, chapter-agnostic tree mirroring the EPUB's own nav structure (arbitrary depth — not hardcoded to Part>Chapter). Top-level array follows spine order; a section with no nav entry of its own (front/back matter the publisher didn't list in the TOC) still appears here as a top-level leaf, classified by `kind` from EPUB landmarks/guide rather than guessed from its content.

spine.description: Flat, ordered list of ids of every section that has its own passages (i.e. every leaf with real reading content), in true linear reading order. This — not array position in `sections`, which mixes in grouping-only nodes like a Part header — is what next/prev navigation, pagination, and audio continuity are keyed to.

notes.description: Flat list of footnote/endnote bodies, referenced by Mark.noteId.

Note.description: A footnote/endnote body. sectionId ties it to the section it belongs to, whether the source EPUB renders notes inline per-section or gathered in one end-of-book section.

text.description: Body text, or alt text when type == "image".

level.description: Set iff type == "heading": the source h1-h6 depth, so the reader can render each distinctly instead of one uniform heading style.

src.description: Set iff type == "image": public path to the extracted asset.

Section.description: A node in the book's content tree — a chapter, a part grouping several chapters, a front/back-matter page, or any nesting depth the source EPUB's own nav actually has. There is no separate 'Chapter' or 'Part' type: a chapter is just a Section with passages and no children; a part is just a Section with children and (usually) no passages of its own. No synthetic numbering is added here — `title` is the nav's own text verbatim, or absent if this section isn't listed in the nav and no confident title exists (never guessed from a heading found inside its body, which was the source of nonsense titles like a Table-of-Contents page's own listing text being mistaken for a chapter title).

kind.description: From EPUB3 landmarks (epub:type) or EPUB2 <guide><reference type>; "unknown" when the source EPUB provides no such classification and this section also isn't reachable from the nav TOC.
